content: Open Safari, navigate to apple.com, and describe what you see on the page

message.content: Open Safari, navigate to apple.com, and describe what you see on the page

content.thinking: The user wants me to open Safari, navigate to apple.com, and describe what I see. I'll use the computer use tool to do this.

content.text: There's a permission dialog asking to allow bash to access the screen. I'll click "Allow" to proceed.
The desktop is visible. Now I'll open Safari by clicking on it in the Dock.
Safari is open showing its Start Page. Now I'll click on the address bar and navigate to apple.com.

The apple.com page has loaded. Here's what I see:

**Apple.com — Current Homepage**

**Hero Section (main featured product):**
- The page is prominently featuring the **iPhone 17e** with the tagline "Feature stacked. Value packed."
- It notes: "Pre-order starting at 6:15 a.m. PT on 3.4 / Available starting 3.11" (March 4 pre-order, March 11 availability)
- Two call-to-action buttons: a solid blue **"Learn more"** and an outlined **"View pricing"**
- A product image shows two iPhone 17e units — one in a light pink/blush color and one in a dark/black color, displayed flat and overlapping

**Navigation Bar:**
The top nav includes the Apple logo and the following product/category links:
- Store, Mac, iPad, iPhone, Watch, Vision, AirPods, TV & Home, Entertainment, Accessories, Support
- Search and shopping bag icons on the right

The page has Apple's characteristic clean, minimal white design aesthetic.
